 {"id":"bd-bxii","title":"Phase 4: TUI (Dashboard, Drill-down, Keyboard Shortcuts)","description":"Phase 4: TUI (Terminal User Interface)

Overview
--------
Build the human interface for reviewing aggregated questions, monitoring
session progress, and making decisions across all repos.

Why TUI?
--------
- Centralized view of all pending questions
- Quick keyboard-driven decisions
- Session monitoring at a glance
- Drill-down for context when needed
- Works over SSH, in tmux, etc.

Components
----------

4.1 Basic Mode (gum-based)
   Fallback when advanced TUI not available:
   - gum style for boxes and formatting
   - gum choose for option selection
   - gum confirm for yes/no
   - Sequential question presentation

4.2 Main Dashboard
   Full-screen TUI with panels:
   - PENDING QUESTIONS: Priority-sorted list
   - ACTIVE SESSIONS: State and progress
   - SUMMARY: Stats and metrics
   - Footer: Keyboard shortcuts

4.3 Drill-down View
   Detailed view for single question:
   - Full context from agent
   - Issue/PR details
   - Patch summary (files changed)
   - Answer options with descriptions
   - Back button to dashboard

4.4 Keyboard Shortcuts
   Efficient keyboard-driven UX:
   - 1-9: Quick answer selection
   - Enter: Expand/collapse
   - d: Drill-down
   - s: Skip current
   - S: Skip all (with confirm)
   - z: Snooze (1d/7d/30d)
   - t: Insert template
   - b: Bulk apply safe changes
   - a: Apply approved changes
   - p/r: Pause/resume
   - q: Quit

4.5 Snooze Feature
   Defer items without skipping permanently:
   - 1 day / 7 days / 30 days
   - Stored in snoozed.json
   - Checked during discovery

4.6 Response Templates
   Pre-configured responses:
   - stale-issue.md
   - duplicate.md
   - needs-info.md
   - wontfix.md
   - thank-you.md

4.7 Bulk Apply
   Apply all low-risk changes that pass gates:
   - Scan plans for risk_level=low
   - Verify tests passed
   - Verify no unanswered questions
   - Confirm before applying

4.8 Patch Summary View
   Show what changed before approving:
   - Branch name
   - Commit count
   - Files changed with diff stats
   - Test status (PASS/FAIL)
   - gh_actions pending

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Dashboard shows all pending questions
- Drill-down provides full context
- Keyboard shortcuts work reliably
- Snooze persists across sessions
- Templates load and apply
- Bulk apply works for safe changes

Estimated Effort
----------------
~600-800 lines of Bash","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:39:36.82324859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:36:05.996826468-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:36:05.996826468-05:00","close_reason":"All major components implemented:

 {"id":"bd-cuq5","title":"Phase 5: Apply Phase (Quality Gates, gh_actions, Push)","description":"Phase 5: Apply Phase

Overview
--------
Execute approved actions from review plans: run quality gates, perform
GitHub mutations (comments, closes, labels), and push changes.

Why Separate Phase?
-------------------
- Plan mode is safe (no mutations)
- User reviews plans before execution
- Quality gates catch issues before push
- Explicit --apply flag required
- Auditability of what was done

Components
----------

5.1 Quality Gates Framework
   Run tests/lint before allowing push:
   - Per-repo policy configuration
   - Auto-detect test commands
   - Fail blocks push
   - Secret scanning

5.2 Per-Repo Policy Configuration
   ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/owner_repo.conf:
   - REVIEW_TEST_CMD
   - REVIEW_LINT_CMD
   - REVIEW_REQUIRE_TESTS
   - Custom validation scripts

5.3 gh_actions Execution
   Execute actions from review-plan.json:
   - comment: gh issue comment / gh pr comment
   - close: gh issue close / gh pr close
   - label: gh issue edit --add-label
   - (merge not supported - policy says no PRs)

5.4 Worktree Merge and Push
   Merge worktree changes to main:
   - Verify quality gates passed
   - Merge worktree branch to base
   - Push to remote
   - Clean up worktree

5.5 Apply Progress Tracking
   Track what has been applied:
   - Mark items as applied in state
   - Log all gh_actions executed
   - Record push timestamps
   - Enable partial retry

5.6 Rollback Capability
   If something goes wrong:
   - Worktrees preserved until explicit cleanup
   - Can revert commits locally
   - gh_actions not reversible (comments stay)
   - Plan artifact serves as audit log

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Quality gates run before push
- gh_actions execute correctly
- Push only with explicit --push flag
- State tracks what was applied
- Rollback path documented

Estimated Effort
----------------
~400 lines of Bash","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:39:37.725040272-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:35:52.053436297-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:35:52.053436297-05:00","close_reason":"All components implemented:
